The root cause is that the binary judge defaults to SKIP whenever the summarizer returns an empty string. With qwen3.5 the thinking tokens consume the entire 120-token budget, so the response strips to empty and every verdict becomes SKIP. That is why recall collapsed to zero for that model family while qwen2.5-coder, which has no thinking preamble, matched model exactly.

I finished the model evaluation. The qwen2.5-coder one-point-five-b model matches the incumbent model model exactly on the labeled oracle: identical accuracy of zero point eight seven, identical recall of zero point nine four, but at 386 milliseconds median latency versus 1475, and only 986 megabytes resident versus 4.7 gigabytes. My recommendation is to swap to qwen2.5-coder.
